source.title: Add Usage Insights freshness/quality dashboard + alerts (lag + reconciliation drift)
source.author: Caleb Johnson
source.description:
Motivation: Usage Insights is going into beta with interactive console queries backed by hourly/day partitions and reconciliation checks against billing. We need a standard dashboard + alert bundle in observability-pack so SRE/on-call can quickly answer: (1) is data fresh, (2) are we dropping/late-ingesting events by route/model, (3) are we drifting vs billing totals beyond tolerance, and (4) are query endpoints timing out for common ranges.

This PR adds a Grafana dashboard and alert rule templates for freshness lag, late event rate, reconciliation drift, and query p95 latency. The dashboard is designed to be imported into customer-facing/internal stacks with minimal per-cluster edits (templated variables for env/region/deployment_mode/workspace_id).

What’s included: 1) Dashboard: “Usage Insights — Data Freshness & Quality” (Grafana JSON) Panels: Aggregation lag (p50/p95) by dataset (route_model_hourly, route_model_daily) and by region; Ingestion late-event ratio (late_events / total_events) by region + streaming endpoint flag; Missing route_tag rate (events where route_template = "unknown" or null); Reconciliation drift (%) between insights totals and billing hook totals, per workspace and overall; Top offenders table: workspaces with highest drift and highest lag; Query API p95 latency + error rate (v1/usage-insights/* endpoints) with annotations for deploys.

2) Alerts (PrometheusRule templates): - UsageInsightsAggregationLagHigh: fires when p95 aggregation lag > 20m for 15m (warn) and > 60m for 10m (page). - UsageInsightsLateEventRatioHigh: fires when late_event_ratio > 2% for 30m (warn), with a separate page threshold at 5% for 15m. - UsageInsightsRouteTagMissing: fires when missing_route_tag_rate > 1% for 30m (warn) and > 3% for 15m (page). - UsageInsightsReconciliationDriftHigh: fires when abs(drift_pct) > 0.5% for 2h (warn) and > 1.0% for 1h (page), excluding small totals (< $50/day) to reduce noise. - UsageInsightsQueryP95Degraded: fires when p95 > 2500ms for 15m (warn) and > 5000ms for 10m (page), scoped to the insights query service.

3) Runbook stub: Adds a runbook page with investigation steps and links to relevant services/datasets (warehouse partitions, ingestion pipeline, billing hook job).

Metric assumptions (coordinated with Telemetry/Analytics): This PR uses the metric names already in-flight on redwood (see ENG-3824/ENG-3817): usage_insights_aggregation_lag_seconds{dataset,region,deployment_mode}; usage_insights_late_event_ratio{region,streaming}; usage_insights_missing_route_tag_rate{region}; usage_insights_reconciliation_drift_pct{workspace_id}; usage_insights_query_request_duration_ms_bucket{route,status}; usage_insights_query_errors_total{route,status}. If any naming changes land before beta, we can follow up with a patch release of observability-pack.

Noise/guardrails: The drift alert includes a minimum spend floor and a 2h lookback to avoid paging on transient rounding/timing skew (e.g., hourly partitions catching up). The lag alert pages only on sustained lag > 60m.

Testing/validation: Imported dashboard into staging Grafana; validated queries on canary metrics in us-east-1 and eu-west-1. Used replayed lag/drift fixtures from staging to verify alert routing and labels.

Rollout notes: This is safe to ship ahead of beta. SREs should import the dashboard to the internal "Console & Tooling" folder and ensure Alertmanager routes page severity=page to #eng-sre, and warn severity=warn to #eng-platform.

Screenshots: (not included in PR; Grafana JSON is the source of truth).
commits: Add Grafana dashboard JSON for Usage Insights freshness/quality
Add PrometheusRule templates for lag, late events, missing route tags, reconciliation drift
Add runbook stub and alert annotations + labels
Tune thresholds + add spend floor for drift paging; reduce noisy grouping
Lint/format dashboards and update pack index
conversation: Allison Grant (review): Dashboard looks good. Can we add a panel for missing route tags specifically for streaming endpoints? We’ve seen region-specific gaps there.
Caleb Johnson (author): Added "Missing route_tag rate (streaming=true)" panel and tied it to the same variable set. Also added an alert that pages only above 3% missing tags to avoid noise.
Logan Wright (review): Drift alert should exclude low-volume workspaces; otherwise small rounding errors will trigger. Also please document tolerance assumptions.
Caleb Johnson (author): Added min_total_usd_per_day floor and documented drift tolerance + lookback in runbook. Alert now uses abs(drift_pct) with a 2h avg.
Cole Summers (review): For reconciliation, make sure the query uses the same rounding semantics (currency minor units) as billing hooks. Otherwise we’ll chase false positives.

Caleb Johnson (author): Updated description in runbook to call out minor-unit rounding and timing skew; dashboard panel now includes both raw drift and drift after rounding-to-cents (if metric exists).
Thomas Baird (review): Aggregation lag panel should show p95 and max; also group by dataset so we can tell hourly vs daily partitions at a glance.
Caleb Johnson (author): Done. Added p95 + max and grouped by dataset with a legend that includes region/deployment_mode.
Marcus Lin (review): Any sensitive labels? workspace_id in drift metrics can be sensitive depending on export path. For alerts, can we include workspace_id only on warn, not on page?

Caleb Johnson (author): Adjusted page-level drift alert to aggregate by env/region only (no workspace_id). Warn alert still includes workspace_id for triage. Added note in runbook about label hygiene.
source.release_notes: Observability pack: Added Usage Insights freshness/quality Grafana dashboard and alert templates for aggregation lag, late event ratio, missing route tags, reconciliation drift, and query latency.

source.title: Add Shadow Traffic quality delta dashboards + scoring lag panels (cardinality-safe)
source.author: Allison Grant
source.release_notes: Adds Grafana dashboards for Shadow Traffic quality deltas and scoring pipeline lag/health. Proposes (not yet enabled) alert rules for regression and lag conditions. All breakdowns are constrained to cardinality-safe label sets per ADR guidance.
source.body:
## Summary
This PR adds a first-cut set of Grafana dashboards to visualize Shadow Traffic quality deltas (control vs shadow variants) and scoring pipeline lag/health. The dashboards are designed for Hosted/Dedicated/Private and intentionally avoid high-cardinality breakdowns (no prompt_id, request_fingerprint, raw route_id, customer_id).

This is intended to support beta oncall readiness and help triage the two main failure classes seen during dogfood/beta:
1) quality deltas missing/stale due to scoring lag or ingestion lag
2) metrics backend pressure due to accidental high-cardinality dimensions

## What’s included
- New "Shadow Traffic / Quality Deltas" dashboard:
  - Δ quality score (shadow - control) over time with confidence bands (when emitted)
  - refusal rate delta, format adherence delta
  - sample size panels (n_control, n_shadow) + effective sample rate
  - breakdowns limited to:
    - deployment_mode (hosted|dedicated|private)
    - model_family (e.g., llama3, qwen2, mistral)
    - route_group (sanitized bucket: "core_chat", "embeddings", "tools", etc.)
    - rubric_version (pinned)
    - experiment_id (capped / top-k only)

- New "Shadow Traffic / Scoring Lag & Worker Health" dashboard:
  - scoring queue lag (p50/p95/p99) + oldest message age
  - worker throughput, retry rate, DLQ count
  - ingestion lag for shadow_pair events
  - circuit breaker open rate + dropped shadow attempts (fail-open)

- Alert rule proposals (NOT enabled by default):
  - scoring pipeline lag SLO breach (ticket -> page escalation guidance in annotations)
  - missing scoring signal (no scored pairs for X minutes while shadow attempts > 0)
  - quality regression advisory alert (requires minimum sample size + CI)

## Cardinality / safety notes
This PR follows the cardinality constraints discussed in SUP-28510 and the Shadow Metrics ADR:
- No dashboards query by prompt_id/request_fingerprint.
- experiment_id panels use a top-k query and default to aggregate view.
- route_id is replaced with route_group generated upstream (low-cardinality mapping maintained by telemetry).
- model_id is avoided; we use model_family + (optional) variant_role (control|shadow).

## Files changed (high level)
- dashboards/shadow_traffic_quality_deltas.jsonnet
- dashboards/shadow_scoring_lag_and_health.jsonnet
- dashboards/_library/shadow_traffic_panels.libsonnet
- alerts/shadow_scoring_lag_rules.yml (proposed)
- alerts/shadow_quality_regression_rules.yml (proposed)
- dashboards/README.md (usage + label guidance)

## Example panel queries (illustrative)
- Quality delta (aggregate):
  sum(rate(redwood_shadow_quality_score_sum{deployment_mode=~"$deployment_mode", rubric_version=~"$rubric_version"}[5m])) / sum(rate(redwood_shadow_quality_score_count{deployment_mode=~"$deployment_mode", rubric_version=~"$rubric_version"}[5m]))
  -
  sum(rate(redwood_control_quality_score_sum{deployment_mode=~"$deployment_mode", rubric_version=~"$rubric_version"}[5m])) / sum(rate(redwood_control_quality_score_count{deployment_mode=~"$deployment_mode", rubric_version=~"$rubric_version"}[5m]))

- Scoring lag (p95):
  histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le, deployment_mode) (rate(redwood_shadow_scoring_lag_seconds_bucket{deployment_mode=~"$deployment_mode"}[5m])))

## Rollout plan
- Merge to observability-pack, dashboards deployed to internal Grafana first (dogfood tenants)
- Enable for Dedicated beta tenants once telemetry labels are confirmed stable (route_group + model_family present)
- Alert rules remain "proposed" until SRE signs off on thresholds and paging policy

## Screenshots
Not included in PR description (dashboards are generated in CI and reviewed via preview JSON in artifacts). See CI artifact: grafana-dashboards-preview.zip

## Checklist
- [x] Panels avoid high-cardinality dimensions
- [x] Default views are aggregate-first; breakdowns are opt-in
- [x] Added dashboard annotations linking to runbook sections (shadow scoring lag / missing data)
- [x] CI renders jsonnet and validates Grafana schema

## Notes / follow-ups
- Follow-up: add a small "label sanitizer" doc with examples of safe/unsafe breakdowns (Logan/Nadia request)
- Follow-up: once confidence interval emission is standardized, switch CI panel to render CI bands instead of placeholder series

Related:
- ENG-5409 (alerting quality regression detection)
- ENG-5410 (cardinality guardrails for shadow metrics)
- SUP-28477 (missing quality dashboard data for Dedicated tenant)
- SUP-28510 (cardinality alerts after enabling shadow traffic)
commits: dashboards: add Shadow Traffic quality delta dashboard skeleton
dashboards: add scoring lag/worker health dashboard
library: factor shared panels + variables for deployment_mode/rubric_version
alerts: add proposed scoring lag + missing signal rules (disabled)
docs: add README guidance on safe breakdowns + top-k constraints
changed_areas: Docs for usage and label guidance
review_conversation:
Logan Wright: Please confirm we’re not using route_id anywhere in template vars. We had a cardinality spike last week when someone added it to a dropdown.
Allison Grant: Confirmed. Only route_group is exposed (and it’s limited to a fixed allowlist upstream). I added a comment in the template vars block and a README note.

Nadia Rahman: For experiment_id breakdown, can we force top-k and show an annotation when the series is truncated?
Allison Grant: Implemented top-k via query + added a dashboard note ("limited to top 10 by sample_count"). Grafana doesn’t give a perfect truncation signal, but we add a stat panel showing total experiments active vs displayed.

Sean Gallagher: Alert rules: can we keep them out of paging until we’ve validated thresholds? Also add runbook links in annotations.
Allison Grant: Kept in alerts/ as proposed; they’re not wired into the deployment bundle yet. Added runbook URLs in rule annotations and dashboard links section.

Selene Huang: For quality regression, ensure minimum sample size gating and avoid triggering on tiny deltas.
Allison Grant: Rule requires n_shadow >= 500 and n_control >= 500 over 30m, plus delta threshold. Also added a panel showing sample sizes next to the delta chart.

Caleb Johnson: CI artifact preview is helpful. Can you add schema validation for Grafana v10 to avoid runtime errors?
Allison Grant: Added grafana-dashboard-validator step (v10) to dashboards/render.

- Alert rule proposals (NOT enabled by default):
  - scoring pipeline lag SLO breach (ticket -> page escalation guidance in annotations)
  - missing scoring signal (no scored pairs for X minutes while shadow attempts > 0)
  - quality regression advisory alert (requires minimum sample size + CI)

## Cardinality / safety notes
This PR follows the cardinality constraints discussed in SUP-28510 and the Shadow Metrics ADR:
- No dashboards query by prompt_id/request_fingerprint.
- experiment_id panels use a top-k query and default to aggregate view.
- route_id is replaced with route_group generated upstream (low-cardinality mapping maintained by telemetry).
- model_id is avoided; we use model_family + (optional) variant_role (control|shadow).

## Files changed (high level)
- dashboards/shadow_traffic_quality_deltas.jsonnet
- dashboards/shadow_scoring_lag_and_health.jsonnet
- dashboards/_library/shadow_traffic_panels.libsonnet
- alerts/shadow_scoring_lag_rules.yml (proposed)
- alerts/shadow_quality_regression_rules.yml (proposed)
- dashboards/README.md (usage + label guidance)

## Example panel queries (illustrative)
- Quality delta (aggregate):
  sum(rate(redwood_shadow_quality_score_sum{deployment_mode=~"$deployment_mode", rubric_version=~"$rubric_version"}[5m])) / sum(rate(redwood_shadow_quality_score_count{deployment_mode=~"$deployment_mode", rubric_version=~"$rubric_version"}[5m]))
  -
  sum(rate(redwood_control_quality_score_sum{deployment_mode=~"$deployment_mode", rubric_version=~"$rubric_version"}[5m])) / sum(rate(redwood_control_quality_score_count{deployment_mode=~"$deployment_mode", rubric_version=~"$rubric_version"}[5m]))

- Scoring lag (p95):
  histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le, deployment_mode) (rate(redwood_shadow_scoring_lag_seconds_bucket{deployment_mode=~"$deployment_mode"}[5m])))

## Rollout plan
- Merge to observability-pack, dashboards deployed to internal Grafana first (dogfood tenants)
- Enable for Dedicated beta tenants once telemetry labels are confirmed stable (route_group + model_family present)
- Alert rules remain "proposed" until SRE signs off on thresholds and paging policy

## Screenshots
Not included in PR description (dashboards are generated in CI and reviewed via preview JSON in artifacts). See CI artifact: grafana-dashboards-preview.zip

## Checklist
- [x] Panels avoid high-cardinality dimensions
- [x] Default views are aggregate-first; breakdowns are opt-in
- [x] Added dashboard annotations linking to runbook sections (shadow scoring lag / missing data)
- [x] CI renders jsonnet and validates Grafana schema

## Notes / follow-ups
- Follow-up: add a small "label sanitizer" doc with examples of safe/unsafe breakdowns (Logan/Nadia request)
- Follow-up: once confidence interval emission is standardized, switch CI panel to render CI bands instead of placeholder series

Related:
- ENG-5409 (alerting quality regression detection)
- ENG-5410 (cardinality guardrails for shadow metrics)
- SUP-28477 (missing quality dashboard data for Dedicated tenant)
- SUP-28510 (cardinality alerts after enabling shadow traffic)

## Summary
This PR adds a first-cut set of Grafana dashboards to visualize Shadow Traffic quality deltas (control vs shadow variants) and scoring pipeline lag/health. The dashboards are designed for Hosted/Dedicated/Private and intentionally avoid high-cardinality breakdowns (no prompt_id, request_fingerprint, raw route_id, customer_id).

This is intended to support beta oncall readiness and help triage the two main failure classes seen during dogfood/beta:
1) quality deltas missing/stale due to scoring lag or ingestion lag
2) metrics backend pressure due to accidental high-cardinality dimensions

## What’s included
- New "Shadow Traffic / Quality Deltas" dashboard:
  - Δ quality score (shadow - control) over time with confidence bands (when emitted)
  - refusal rate delta, format adherence delta
  - sample size panels (n_control, n_shadow) + effective sample rate
  - breakdowns limited to:
    - deployment_mode (hosted|dedicated|private)
    - model_family (e.g., llama3, qwen2, mistral)
    - route_group (sanitized bucket: "core_chat", "embeddings", "tools", etc.)
    - rubric_version (pinned)
    - experiment_id (capped / top-k only)

- New "Shadow Traffic / Scoring Lag & Worker Health" dashboard:
  - scoring queue lag (p50/p95/p99) + oldest message age
  - worker throughput, retry rate, DLQ count
  - ingestion lag for shadow_pair events
  - circuit breaker open rate + dropped shadow attempts (fail-open)

source.title: Add incident taxonomy dashboards + alerting (type/owner breakdowns + playbook links)
source.author: Sergio Costa
source.release_notes:
- New Grafana dashboard: Incident Taxonomy (v1) with breakdowns by incident_type and owner_team
- New alert rules: high unclassified rate, incident spike by type/owner, and sustained active incidents by tier
- Standardized playbook/runbook links emitted as dashboard annotations and panel links
source.body:
## Summary
This PR adds the initial Incident Taxonomy (v1) dashboards + a small set of alert rules, per the reliability program initiative. The goal is to make taxonomy classification measurable and to enable reporting/triage by incident_type + owner_team without exploding metric label cardinality.

Key deliverables in this PR:
- Grafana dashboard: **Incident Taxonomy (v1)**
  - incident volume and impact proxy panels (by tier, severity)
  - breakdowns by **incident_type** and **owner_team**
  - classification compliance (% unknown/unclassified)
  - MTTA/MTTR approximations using incident event timestamps
  - drilldowns from type -> owner -> recent incidents (table)
  - playbook links based on incident_type (panel links + table column)
- Prometheus rules:
  - recording rules to normalize/aggregate incident event counters into a stable set of labels
  - alert rules for (1) unclassified rate regression (2) spike of incidents by type/owner (3) sustained active incidents for Dedicated/Private

## Context / Motivation
As part of the Reliability Program (“Incident taxonomy + ownership mapping”), incident_type/subtype and owner are now required fields in incident tooling. We need dashboards/alerts that:
- show whether classification is being used correctly (and where it’s not)
- create a feedback loop for top recurring incident categories
- allow on-call to quickly jump from “what’s happening” -> “who owns it” -> “what runbook should I follow”

This PR targets the spec in Confluence (Incident taxonomy dashboards spec) and ties into Linear ENG-4828.

## Design notes (cardinality + data sources)
Data sources used:
- `incident_events_total{event=...}` emitted from incident-bot pipeline / annotations ingestion
- existing `request_errors_total` and `request_latency_seconds` are NOT relabeled here; the dashboard focuses on incidents as the unit of analysis

Cardinality constraints:
- `incident_type` is treated as a strict enum (v1 allowlist)
- `owner_team` is constrained to a small allowlist of on-call teams (e.g., SRE, Fleet, Routing, Runtime, Observability, SecurityOps, PrivateDeploy)
- `incident_subtype` is intentionally not used as a Prometheus label in this PR (shown only in the incident table sourced from annotations/events)
- `unknown` is a first-class bucket to avoid missing data (explicitly tracked)

## What changed
### Dashboards
- New dashboard: `dashboards/incident-taxonomy-v1.jsonnet`
  - Variables: `tier`, `severity`, `owner_team`, `incident_type`, `env`, `region`
  - Panels:
    - “Incidents created (last 24h/7d) by incident_type”
    - “Incidents created by owner_team”
    - “Active incidents over time” (stacked by tier)
    - “Classification compliance” (% known vs unknown)
    - “Top incident types (28d) + median time-to-mitigate”
    - “Recent incidents” table (links to Slack channel + incident doc + runbook)
  - Playbook links:
    - Uses a small mapping file to link type -> runbook URL (SRE/Infra/Runtime runbooks)

### Prometheus rules
- `prometheus/rules/incident_taxonomy_recording_rules.yaml`
  - Normalizes `incident_events_total` into:
    - `ri:incidents_created:count{tier,severity,incident_type,owner_team}`
    - `ri:incidents_active:count{tier,severity,incident_type,owner_team}`
    - `ri:incidents_unclassified:count{tier,severity}`
- `prometheus/alerts/incident_taxonomy_alerts.yaml`
  - `IncidentTaxonomyUnclassifiedRateHigh` (warn): unclassified >= 10% over 6h (all tiers)
  - `IncidentSpikeByTypeOwner` (warn): 3x baseline over 2h for any (incident_type, owner_team) in Hosted
  - `SustainedActiveIncidentsDedicatedPrivate` (page): active incidents >= 2 for 30m for Dedicated/Private

## Example queries (for reviewers)
- Incidents created by type:
  - `sum by (incident_type) (increase(ri:incidents_created:count{tier="$tier"}[24h]))`
- Unclassified rate:
  - `sum(increase(ri:incidents_unclassified:count{tier="$tier"}[6h])) / sum(increase(ri:incidents_created:count{tier="$tier"}[6h]))`

## Screenshots
(Attached in PR UI)
- Incident Taxonomy (v1) overview
- Drilldown: Routing-owned incidents, last 14d
- Classification compliance panel with unknown bucket

## Rollout / Ops
- Safe to deploy behind existing dashboard folder permissions.
- Alert routing:
  - warn alerts to `#eng-sre` (triage)
  - page alerts to `#incidents` + paging policy for SRE primary (owner_team is informational; paging by owner will be part of ENG-4823)

## Testing
- `make lint`
- `make dashboards-test` (jsonnet render)
- `promtool test rules`
- Verified panels render in staging Grafana (sre-observability)

## Follow-ups (not in this PR)
- Reduce/lock down any remaining label surfaces if we see unexpected churn in `owner_team` values (see review notes).
- Add saved views per tier/team once CS permissions model is finalized.
- Add explicit MTTR SLA breach overlays once remediation SLA timers land (ENG-4829).

---
Closes: ENG-4828
commits: dashboards: add Incident Taxonomy (v1) dashboard with type/owner drilldowns
rules: add incident taxonomy recording rules and initial alert set
dashboards: add playbook link mapping + panel links
chore: add promtool tests + update dashboard index
review_conversation:
Allison Grant (review): Concerned about owner_team cardinality. Can we enforce an allowlist here and map unknowns to 'unknown' rather than creating new label values? Also, please avoid adding incident_subtype as a metric label.

Sergio Costa (author): Agree. Updated recording rule to apply an allowlist transform and map anything else to owner_team="unknown". Left incident_subtype out of metrics; subtype remains in the incident table only.

Allison Grant: Thanks. Please also add a panel to make unclassified/unknown visible so we can drive adoption.

Sergio Costa: Added “Classification compliance” panel + alert on sustained unclassified rate.

Aisha Bello (review): MTTR panel: what timestamps are you using? We should be explicit this is based on incident events (created/mitigated/resolved) and not request-level SLO windows.

Sergio Costa (author): Good call. Updated panel titles + dashboard description to call out event-based timings. Added a tooltip note and linked to the incident taxonomy dashboard spec.

Rafael Mendes (review): Naming: can we stick to `Incident Taxonomy (v1)` and use `incident_type` values exactly as the taxonomy doc (e.g., ROUTING_FALLBACK_MISBEHAVIOR vs ROUTING_FALLBACK)? Also please add runbook links for top-level categories.

Sergio Costa (author): Renamed dashboard + aligned enums to v1 taxonomy list. Added playbook mapping file with links for Availability/Overload, Latency Regression, Streaming Disconnects, Routing/Fallback, Fleet/Capacity, Control Plane, Auth/RBAC, Telemetry Blind Spots, Private Connectivity.

Ethan Park (review): The recent incidents table links to the control plane incident doc endpoint; make sure the URL is stable across envs. Also please avoid exposing edit links in CS-visible views.

Sergio Costa (author): Switched to the read-only incident view URL and gated edit links behind internal-only variable. (CS permissions work is separate; this keeps the dashboard safe by default.)

source.title: perf-canary: add warning-level alert rules (dampening + min sample size) routed to #eng-runtime
source.author: Allison Grant
source.release_notes: Adds warning-only perf-canary alerts for per-model p95 latency regressions and tokens/sec drops with dampening + minimum sample guardrails; routes to #eng-runtime (no paging).
source.body:
## Summary
Adds the initial set of warning-level perf-canary alerts (no paging) to detect per-model serving performance regressions early, with explicit guardrails to reduce noise during ramp.

This PR wires:
- Warning alerts for p95 latency regression and tokens/sec drops
- Dampening (sustained windows) + min-sample conditions
- Alertmanager routing to Slack #eng-runtime
- Runbook + dashboard links in annotations

This is intended to be enabled first in us-east with a limited model list (per ENG-4880 rollout plan). Paging rules are tracked separately (pr-19183).

## Motivation / context
During early perf-canary rollout we saw false positives driven by low volume, baseline drift, and transient telemetry delays. The warning rules in this PR aim to be:
- Low-noise: only fire when there’s enough data
- Actionable: include baseline vs observed deltas + direct links
- Safe: warning-only channel routing while we calibrate

## Alerting approach (v0)
### What we alert on
1) **p95 end-to-end latency regression** (primary): observed p95 vs baseline p95
2) **tokens/sec drop** (secondary): observed tokens/sec vs baseline tokens/sec

### Key guardrails
- **Minimum sample size**: require >= N completed canary requests in the evaluation window
- **Dampening**: require condition to hold for a continuous duration before firing
- **Cardinality limits**: alert labels only include model + region + tier + runtime_build_id (no prompt-shape label)

### Windows / thresholds (v0 defaults)
- p95 latency regression: >= +12% for 15m (warning)
- tokens/sec drop: <= -10% for 15m (warning)
- min samples: >= 200 requests in 15m per (model, region, tier)

These defaults are intentionally conservative; per-model overrides will be managed via the baseline/threshold config in the perf-canary service and/or follow-up PR if we need rule-level overrides.

## Implementation details
- Adds alerting rules under `alerts/perf_canary/warnings.yaml`
- Adds an Alertmanager route/receiver for `slack_eng_runtime` with a grouping key on (alertname, model, region, tier)
- Adds templates used to render compact Slack messages including baseline/observed/threshold and deep links

### Example rule snippet
The core pattern is:

- Ensure sufficient volume:
  `sum(increase(perf_canary_requests_total{status="ok"}[15m])) by (model, region, tier) >= 200`

- Compare observed p95 to baseline p95 (both expressed in ms):
  `histogram_quantile(0.95, sum(rate(perf_canary_e2e_latency_ms_bucket[5m])) by (le, model, region, tier)) / on(model, region, tier) group_left perf_canary_baseline_p95_latency_ms`

- Fire when ratio exceeds threshold for long enough:
  `> 1.12 for: 15m`

Annotations include:
- `runbook_url`: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SRE/Runbook%3A+Perf+Regression+Canary+Alert
- `dashboard_url`: https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/perf-canary/perf-canary?var-model={{ $labels.model }}&var-region={{ $labels.region }}

## Testing
- `promtool check rules` in CI
- `promtool test rules` added/updated for:
  - happy path: regression sustained with min samples -> fires
  - low-volume path: regression but samples < min -> does not fire
  - transient spike: regression for < for-window -> does not fire
- Alertmanager config validation

## Rollout plan
1) Merge rules disabled by default (gated by `perf_canary_alerts_enabled` config in the alerting pipeline)
2) Enable in us-east for 2–3 models with only warning routing
3) Tune thresholds/min-samples if needed (ENG-4882)
4) Add paging rules separately once warning noise is acceptable (pr-19183)

## Risks / mitigations
- Risk: telemetry ingestion delay causes late datapoints and apparent regressions
  - Mitigation: long `for:` window + min samples; dashboard links include ingestion-lag panel (from pr-19174)
- Risk: baseline drift causes slow burn alerts
  - Mitigation: warning-only; follow-up ADR work for baseline update policy

## Screenshots
N/A (rule-based); see CI output for promtool rule tests.

## Checklist
- [x] Warning alerts only (no paging)
- [x] Min sample size guardrails
- [x] Dampening windows
- [x] Slack routing to #eng-runtime
- [x] Runbook + dashboard links
- [x] promtool validation + tests

- Dashboards: pr-19174
- Paging rules: pr-19183
- Perf-canary payload formatting: perf-canary pr-19244
commits: alerts(perf-canary): add warning rules for latency regression + tokens/sec drop
alerts(perf-canary): add min-sample guardrails and sustained-for dampening
alertmanager: route perf-canary warnings to #eng-runtime receiver
templates: add perf-canary slack formatting with dashboard/runbook links
tests: add promtool rule tests for low-volume + transient spike cases
ci: wire promtool rule validation job for perf-canary alert rules
conversation: Rafael Mendes (review): Can we confirm these are warning-only and won’t hit paging routes via default receivers? Also, can we group by model+region+tier to avoid spam when multiple series flap.

Allison Grant (author): Confirmed warning-only: severity=warning + route match requires `team=runtime` and `notify=slack_only`. No pager receiver in this chain. I updated grouping to (alertname, model, region, tier) and set `group_interval=10m` to reduce thread spam.
Noah Patel (review): Thresholds: +12% p95 and -10% tps seem fine for v0. Do we have a min-sample rationale? 200/15m might be tight for smaller regions during off-peak.

Allison Grant (author): Good call. I chose 200 based on week-1 variance notes from staging + the goal to avoid low-volume noise. We can drop to 120 for eu-west once we see real volume; I’d prefer to start conservative in us-east. Added a TODO + linked ENG-4882 for tuning and made min-samples a constant in the rule file so it’s easy to change.

Logan Wright (review): Please double-check we’re not including any high-cardinality labels (prompt_shape, kernel_id) on the alert labels themselves. It’s fine in dashboards but not in Alertmanager labels.

Allison Grant (author): Adjusted: alert labels now only include model, region, tier. runtime_build_id moved into annotations (rendered from the recording rule) to keep grouping stable. prompt_shape explicitly dropped via `without(prompt_shape)` in the recording query.
Aisha Bello (review): +1. Please add an annotation for queueing-vs-runtime breakdown dashboard and include the runbook link in both rules.
Allison Grant (author): Added `breakdown_dashboard_url` and ensured `runbook_url` is present on all perf-canary warning alerts.
Rafael Mendes (approval): Approved. Routing + grouping changes address my concerns. Let’s keep this warning-only until ENG-4880 signoff.
Noah Patel (approval): Approved. Conservative defaults make sense for phase 1; we’ll tune after a week of data.
Logan Wright (approval): Approved after label-cardinality change.
source.merge_outcome: Merged via squash into main by Allison Grant

title: perf-canary: add warning-level alert rules (dampening + min sample size) routed to #eng-runtime
preview:
## Summary
Adds the initial set of warning-level perf-canary alerts (no paging) to detect per-model serving performance regressions early, with explicit guardrails to reduce noise during ramp.

This PR wires:
- Warning alerts for p95 latency regression and tokens/sec drops
- Dampening (sustained windows) + min-sample conditions
- Alertmanager routing to Slack #eng-runtime
- Runbook + dashboard links in annotations

This is intended to be enabled first in us-east with a limited model list (per ENG-4880 rollout plan). Paging rules are tracked separately (pr-19183).

## Motivation / context
During early perf-canary rollout we saw false positives driven by low volume, baseline drift, and transient telemetry delays. The warning rules in this PR aim to be:
- Low-noise: only fire when there’s enough data
- Actionable: include baseline vs observed deltas + direct links
- Safe: warning-only channel routing while we calibrate

## Alerting approach (v0)
### What we alert on
1) **p95 end-to-end latency regression** (primary): observed p95 vs baseline p95
2) **tokens/sec drop** (secondary): observed tokens/sec vs baseline tokens/sec

### Key guardrails
- **Minimum sample size**: require >= N completed canary requests in the evaluation window
- **Dampening**: require condition to hold for a continuous duration before firing
- **Cardinality limits**: alert labels only include model + region + tier + runtime_build_id (no prompt-shape label)

### Windows / thresholds (v0 defaults)
- p95 latency regression: >= +12% for 15m (warning)
- tokens/sec drop: <= -10% for 15m (warning)
- min samples: >= 200 requests in 15m per (model, region, tier)

These defaults are intentionally conservative; per-model overrides will be managed via the baseline/threshold config in the perf-canary service and/or follow-up PR if we need rule-level overrides.

## Implementation details
- Adds alerting rules under `alerts/perf_canary/warnings.yaml`
- Adds an Alertmanager route/receiver for `slack_eng_runtime` with a grouping key on (alertname, model, region, tier)
- Adds templates used to render compact Slack messages including baseline/observed/threshold and deep links

### Example rule snippet
The core pattern is:

- Ensure sufficient volume:
  `sum(increase(perf_canary_requests_total{status="ok"}[15m])) by (model, region, tier) >= 200`

- Compare observed p95 to baseline p95 (both expressed in ms):
  `histogram_quantile(0.95, sum(rate(perf_canary_e2e_latency_ms_bucket[5m])) by (le, model, region, tier)) / on(model, region, tier) group_left perf_canary_baseline_p95_latency_ms`

- Fire when ratio exceeds threshold for long enough:
  `> 1.12 for: 15m`

Annotations include:
- `runbook_url`: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SRE/Runbook%3A+Perf+Regression+Canary+Alert
- `dashboard_url`: https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/perf-canary/perf-canary?var-model={{ $labels.model }}&var-region={{ $labels.region }}

## Testing
- `promtool check rules` in CI
- `promtool test rules` added/updated for:
  - happy path: regression sustained with min samples -> fires
  - low-volume path: regression but samples < min -> does not fire
  - transient spike: regression for < for-window -> does not fire
- Alertmanager config validation

## Rollout plan
1) Merge rules disabled by default (gated by `perf_canary_alerts_enabled` config in the alerting pipeline)
2) Enable in us-east for 2–3 models with only warning routing
3) Tune thresholds/min-samples if needed (ENG-4882)
4) Add paging rules separately once warning noise is acceptable (pr-19183)

## Risks / mitigations
- Risk: telemetry ingestion delay causes late datapoints and apparent regressions
  - Mitigation: long `for:` window + min samples; dashboard links include ingestion-lag panel (from pr-19174)
- Risk: baseline drift causes slow burn alerts
  - Mitigation: warning-only; follow-up ADR work for baseline update policy

## Screenshots
N/A (rule-based); see CI output for promtool rule tests.

## Checklist
- [x] Warning alerts only (no paging)
- [x] Min sample size guardrails
- [x] Dampening windows
- [x] Slack routing to #eng-runtime
- [x] Runbook + dashboard links
- [x] promtool validation + tests

Related:
- Dashboards: pr-19174
- Paging rules: pr-19183
- Perf-canary payload formatting: perf-canary pr-19244

source.title: Perf baseline dashboards + alert rules for representative suite (TTFT/TPOT/throughput/variance) with manifest links
source.author: Sergio Costa
source.description: Context: As part of the Q1'26 perf baseline + representative workload suite, we need a standard set of dashboards and alerting that (a) make baseline runs explorable, (b) make regressions actionable, and (c) are attributable via a first-class link from the panel to the run manifest + trace artifacts. This PR adds a versioned dashboard bundle and alert rules aimed at baseline runs + perf-canary outputs. What’s included: 1) Grafana dashboards (folder: "Perf Baselines") with panels for TTFT/TPOT percentiles, throughput, saturation proxies, and variance/noise views. 2) Alert rules for baseline regressions (non-paging by default) and canary gating signals (paging for release-branch gating only) with routing to the right channels/oncall. 3) Standard run label/templating conventions aligned with ADR-0172 (model, model_family, workload_id, region, sku, runtime_git_sha, scheduler_profile, kernel_profile, cache_mode, run_id). 4) Panel links: dashboard → run manifest (JSON) → trace bundle (if present). Links are built from run_id + artifact_base_url labels so we don’t have to hardcode per-environment paths. 5) A small metrics dictionary excerpt in-repo to keep dashboards stable while the full dictionary draft is iterated in Drive. Dashboards: - "Perf Baseline — Overview": top-line TTFT/TPOT p50/p95/p99 and throughput for the selected run cohort; quick filters for region/SKU/model/workload/cache_mode; includes a "cold vs warm" compare row. - "Perf Baseline — Latency detail": TTFT and TPOT with per-stage breakdown (client boundary vs server boundary where available), plus queue_wait_ms and batch_assembly_ms panels when the runtime emits them. - "Perf Baseline — Throughput & saturation": tokens/s, req/s (embeddings), GPU utilization (if present), batch size distribution, and a "saturation heuristics" row (queue depth, shed counters, backpressure). - "Perf Baseline — Variance": run-to-run variability panels (median absolute deviation + coefficient of variation) and a "noise budget" view to help separate regressions from fleet variability; includes warmup window visualization based on run_phase label. - "Perf Baseline — Regression compare": diff view against a selected baseline anchor (baseline_run_id) with percent deltas for p95/p99 TTFT/TPOT and throughput; flags when deltas exceed configured thresholds. Alerts: - perf_baseline_regression_warning: triggers when p99 TTFT or p99 TPOT regresses > 5% vs baseline anchor for a given (model, workload, region, sku, cache_mode) cohort, sustained over N=5 samples (configurable). Routes to #eng-runtime-perf-triage and tags the cohort in the alert annotations. - perf_baseline_variance_spike: triggers when variance (CV) exceeds threshold, indicating fleet instability; routes to #eng-sre-benchmark-fleet (non-paging). - perf_canary_gating_failure: only enabled for release branches (via label enable_gating=true); pages runtime oncall if p99 TTFT/TPOT regresses beyond gating thresholds for required cohorts. Notes on measurement boundaries: Dashboards assume TTFT/TPOT are server-side timings emitted by the benchmark harness/runtime instrumentation (not client wall-clock). We still show "first-token-on-wire" when present, but it’s not used for gating alerts. Manifest/trace linkouts: Panel links use a templated URL: ${artifact_base_url}/runs/${run_id}/manifest.json and ${artifact_base_url}/runs/${run_id}/traces/ (the trace link is conditional; shows as "Trace artifacts" with a warning when the label trace_available=false). Rollout plan: Merge behind folder-level RBAC (Engineering only). Alerts ship in disabled-by-default state for 48h dry run in staging; then enable warning alerts in prod; gating/paging remains controlled via enable_gating label and release branch policy. Testing: Validated dashboard JSON imports cleanly on grafana-staging and prod sandbox. Verified alert expressions against 2 historical baseline runs (chat_streaming and long_prefill) and confirmed annotation links resolve to manifest artifacts from benchmark-lab uploads. Screenshots: Attached in PR comments (overview + variance panel examples).
commits: Add baseline dashboard folder + initial overview dashboard (TTFT/TPOT/throughput)
Add latency detail dashboard with queue/batch instrumentation panels
Add variance dashboard (CV/MAD) and regression compare view vs baseline_run_id
Add alert rules for baseline regressions, variance spikes, and release gating failures
Normalize perf suite labels + templated links to run manifests and traces
Docs: importing dashboards + alert enablement and routing notes
review_conversation: Allison Grant: Can we align label keys with ADR-0172 exactly? Right now I see modelFamily in a couple panels. Also please ensure we don’t gate on first-token-on-wire since that includes client effects.

Sergio Costa (author): Updated panels to use model_family everywhere and added a note in the dashboard description about server-side TTFT/TPOT vs first-token-on-wire. Alerts only reference server-side series.
Aisha Bello: For variance alerts, can we make routing non-paging and land it in the benchmark fleet channel? Also add an annotation that suggests checking GPU clock locking / placement variance.
Sergio Costa (author): Done. variance_spike routes to #eng-sre-benchmark-fleet and includes a runbook link + annotation hints (clock locking, warmup, placement).
Sean Gallagher: Please gate paging alert activation behind an enable_gating label or a release-branch selector; we don’t want this paging mainline nightly until thresholds are stable.
Sergio Costa (author): Added enable_gating label requirement + documented enablement. Default is disabled. Paging only happens when enable_gating=true.
Rina Kobayashi: Manifest linkouts look good. Can we also include run_id + runtime_git_sha in the alert title so it’s searchable in Slack? We’ve been struggling to correlate to artifacts quickly.
Sergio Costa (author): Updated alert title + added annotations for run_id/runtime_git_sha and direct manifest link.
Allison Grant: Approving pending one change: please add a minimal recording rule to stabilize panel queries (reduce cardinality impact).
Sergio Costa (author): Added perf-suite recording rules for p50/p95/p99 TTFT/TPOT and throughput by cohort. Re-ran validation.
Allison Grant: Approved.
Aisha Bello: Approved.
Sean Gallagher: Approved.
source.release_notes: Internal: Added Perf Baselines Grafana dashboards (TTFT/TPOT/throughput/variance) and baseline regression alert rules with run manifest + trace linkouts; paging is disabled by default and can be enabled for release gating via enable_gating=true.
related_links: Linear ENG-4828 (perf-canary integration and regression thresholds)
ADR-0172 (benchmark metrics and run manifest format)

source.title: Add hot-route protection dashboards + alert rules (enterprise tier + shed reasons)
source.author: Sergio Costa
source.release_notes: Adds dashboard + alerting package for Hot-route Capacity Protection: tier-aware traffic/429s, shed reasons, queue-depth signals, and enterprise SLO burn panels.
source.body: Context: As part of Hot-route Capacity Protection (ENG-4926), SRE needs a consistent set of dashboards + alert rules for protected routes (/v1/chat/completions, /v1/embeddings, /v1/rerank) that make it easy to answer: (1) are enterprise routes staying within SLO under fleet pressure, (2) are we shedding/throttling the right tier first, and (3) what is the reason breakdown (quota vs overload vs admission control) when 429s occur. This PR adds Grafana dashboard definitions (jsonnet) and Prometheus alert rules for the new metrics emitted by gateway/routing/runtime (see redwood PRs for instrumentation and 429 reason codes). Scope: dashboards (per-route/per-tier latency + error + shed), annotations for protection activation events, and paging rules focused on enterprise degradation (not expected best-effort shedding). What’s included: (A) Dashboards: 1) Hot Route Protection – Overview: request volume by route group + tier, 429 rate by reason, shed/admission-control rate, queue depth / scheduler lag signals, and 5xx. 2) Hot Route Protection – Enterprise SLO: p50/p95/p99 latency by protected route group for enterprise tier, availability (2xx/3xx) and error budget burn panels, with burn windows (5m/1h/6h). 3) Hot Route Protection – Debug: top tenants (sampled/low-cardinality) by route group, saturation indicators, and correlation panels for policy-eval latency + routing decision outcomes. (B) Alerts (Prometheus rules): 1) Page: enterprise protected route latency burn > 2x for 15m OR fast burn > 10x for 5m (gated to enterprise tier + protected route groups). 2) Page: enterprise protected route 5xx rate > 1% for 10m (excluding client cancellations). 3) Ticket: sustained overload protection activation (shed_rate_overload_reason > threshold for 30m) when enterprise SLO remains healthy (expected during pressure) to drive capacity follow-up without paging storms. 4) Ticket: missing/flatlined metrics for new overload reason series (helps catch ingestion regressions and the cardinality hotfix class of issues). Notes/guardrails: All queries follow the overload metrics cardinality guidance: no tenant_id in primary panels; we use route_group, tier, region, reason_code only. The “Debug” dashboard includes an opt-in variable for sampled tenant (uses pre-aggregated topK series). Alert rules intentionally avoid paging on best-effort 429 spikes alone; we page only when enterprise SLO signals degrade. Rollout: dashboards are safe to merge before full multi-region enablement; panels will show ‘No data’ until metrics are present. Alert rules are shipped disabled by default via env toggle in the pack; enablement to follow GA checklist (eng-releases thread + canary results). Links: Linear ENG-4926 (dashboards/alerts), ENG-4927 (incident-bot notifications). Related: slo-toolkit PR for standard burn alert helper functions, redwood PR adding overload reason codes to 429s, and runtime PR exporting queue depth + scheduler lag.
changed_areas: README.md (new section: Hot-route protection dashboards + enablement notes)
illustrative_snippets: Alert rule (excerpt): alert: EnterpriseProtectedRouteLatencyBurnFast expr: (slo:burn_rate_5m{service="hosted-api",slo="protected_route_latency",tier="enterprise"} > 10) and on(region) (overload:protection_active{tier="enterprise"} >= 0) for: 5m labels: severity: page annotations: summary: "Fast burn on enterprise protected route latency"
Panel query (excerpt): sum by (region, route_group, tier, reason_code) (rate(api_gateway_requests_total{status="429",route_group=~"protected_.*"}[5m]))
commits: b3d2e1a hot-route-protection: add overview dashboard with tier + reason breakdown
c11a9bf hot-route-protection: add enterprise SLO dashboard with burn panels
8a90c4d alerts: add enterprise latency/5xx paging rules + nonpaging overload activation ticket rule
0e7d8aa lib: consolidate overload reason label mapping + query helpers
9c6f1de docs: add enablement/rollout notes + cardinality guidance for debug dashboard
conversation: Allison Grant: Please confirm the tier label source of truth. In some places we used customer_tier in older dashboards. We should standardize on tier to match the metrics standard doc. Also, can we annotate protection activation events on the enterprise SLO dashboard so on-call can correlate quickly?
Sergio Costa (author): Good catch. Updated all queries to tier= (removed customer_tier). Added annotations driven by overload:protection_active transitions (per region + route_group).

Aisha Bello: Paging on 5xx > 1% for 10m seems high/low depending on route; do we have route-specific exceptions? Also, can we gate the paging rule to exclude errors when request volume is tiny (avoid noise in low-traffic regions)?

Sergio Costa (author): Added min_request_rate guard (>= 5 rps aggregate over 10m) and left route-specific thresholds out for now per our goal of low policy complexity. If we need exceptions later, we can add an allowlist by route_group in this pack.

Logan Wright: The debug dashboard includes top tenants — are we sure the query doesn’t accidentally explode series? Please ensure it only hits pre-aggregated topK and doesn’t expose tenant_id by default.
Sergio Costa (author): Adjusted to use overload:top_tenants_by_route_group (pre-aggregated) and made tenant variable empty by default. Added README warning + link to cardinality guidance.

Rafael Mendes: Looks good. Minor: can we rename ‘shed’ panel to ‘admission control / shed’ to match runbook terminology? Also, please add a non-paging alert for missing metrics so we catch ingestion regressions early.
Sergio Costa (author): Done — panel titles updated, and added HotRouteProtectionMetricsMissing as a ticket-level alert.
Allison Grant: Approved.
Aisha Bello: Approved with the min_rps guard.
Rafael Mendes: Approved. Please coordinate enablement with GA checklist.
follow_ups: Enable alert rules in us-east canary after GA go/no-go (ENG-4926 checklist item)
Add panel for streaming termination semantics once gateway emits stream_terminated_reason (ties to SUP-18462 follow-up)
Revisit thresholds after EU rollout once we have 2+ weeks of burn history (post-launch metrics review ENG-4931)

source.title: Add activation funnel dashboard panels + suggested alerts for regressions
source.author: Sergio Costa
source.body:
Context: This is part of the Lifecycle Growth Experiments sprint (Hosted API activation + early retention). We need a standardized, self-serve dashboard that answers: (1) where do new signups drop in the funnel, (2) which experiment cohorts/variants moved the needle, and (3) how do we catch regressions quickly after email/nudge/retargeting launches.

What’s included in this PR: Adds a set of activation funnel panels to the existing Growth/PLG dashboard pack and wires in suggested (disabled-by-default) alert rules. The dashboard focuses on signup -> API key created -> first successful request -> first 100k tokens within 7d, plus TTFT distributions.

Dashboard updates: - New row: “Activation funnel (new signups)” with conversion panels per step (absolute counts + rates), and a time series of overall activation_rate_7d (first_request within 7d of signup). - Breakdown panels: conversion by region, SDK (ts/python/rest), and experiment cohort (feature flag holdout vs treatment). - Variant drilldowns: email_variant_id, nudge_id, and utm_campaign (for retargeting) as optional breakdown dimensions (these are behind dashboard variables to avoid exploding cardinality by default). - Latency: p50/p95 TTFT for first successful request (filtered to first_request events only) with a note about sampling and outlier handling. - Data quality: panel that tracks event ingestion gaps (% missing region, % missing sdk) and a warning annotation if the dedupe-fix version hasn’t fully rolled out.

Suggested alerts (added as templates, not auto-enabled): - Activation regression: activation_rate_7d drops >15% vs 28d baseline for 2h (with minimum N guardrail). - Funnel step regression: api_key_created_rate and first_request_rate drops separately, to help localize which step is broken. - TTFT regression: p95 TTFT for first request increases >25% vs baseline (suppressed if volume is low).

Notes/assumptions: - Queries expect the standardized event taxonomy from ENG-10492 (signup/api_key_created/first_request/first_100k_tokens) and will be most accurate once the duplicate-event emission fix is fully deployed (see redwood hotfix PR referenced in ENG-10492 notes). - All breakdowns default to low-cardinality dimensions; high-cardinality drilldowns are opt-in via variables.

Testing: - jsonnet render + schema validation via `make dashboards` - alert rule linting via `make alerts-lint` - manual spot-check in Grafana: panels load under 2s for last 7d; breakdowns under 5s with variables enabled.

Rollout: No runtime impact. Once merged, dashboards ship with the next observability-pack sync. Alert templates require explicit enablement per env (stage/prod) and per oncall ownership.

Screenshots: included in the PR conversation (Grafana panel previews + variable dropdowns).
commits: dashboards: add activation funnel row + variables for cohort/variant
queries: normalize funnel step calculations + volume guardrails
alerts: add suggested activation + TTFT regression rules (disabled by default)
docs: annotate panel definitions and data-quality caveats
source.conversation:
Allison Grant (review): Can we make it explicit that these alert rules are templates and not enabled by default? Also please rename “Activation (7d)” to “First request within 7d (activation)” to match the KPI doc.

Sergio Costa (author): Yep — updated alert README to call out disabled-by-default + added “enablement checklist” section. Renamed the panel + variable description to match KPI naming.

Logan Wright (review): The cohort breakdown should use `experiment_cohort` from the flag service, not inferred from timestamps. Otherwise holdouts drift when we re-run assignment.

Sergio Costa (author): Good catch. Switched cohort breakdown to `experiment_cohort` and added a fallback note (panel shows “unknown” if not present) so we notice if event properties are missing.

Mei Lin (review): Query perf: the `utm_campaign` breakdown can get heavy. Can we default it off and require enabling via variable?

Sergio Costa (author): Done — it’s now an opt-in variable, and the default breakdown panel uses region/sdk only.

Paula Mendes (review): Add a minimum-volume threshold for the regression alert, otherwise we’ll page on low-traffic hours.

Sergio Costa (author): Added `min_requests >= 200` guardrail and a note in the alert template.

Allison Grant (final): Approving. Please make sure the data quality panel includes % missing sdk + region (those are the two most common issues we’ve seen).

Sergio Costa (author): Added both; merged after green CI.
source.release_notes: Adds activation funnel dashboard panels (signup → API key → first request → first 100k tokens) with cohort/variant drilldowns and suggested alert templates for activation/TTFT regressions.

source.title: Add rollback dashboard panels + annotations for Optimization Config apply/promote/rollback events
source.author: Allison Grant
source.description: Summary: This PR updates the "Optimization Rollout Health" Grafana dashboard in observability-pack to make safe apply + rollback operations visible and diagnosable during canaries and incident response. It adds (1) dashboard annotations for Optimization Config lifecycle events (apply, promote, rollback, gate pause/fail) and (2) panels that slice the core health signals by optimization_config_version to quickly confirm whether the rollout is causing elevated error/latency or saturation. Motivation: During the gameday drill and a recent customer escalation (SUP-18421) we spent too long correlating p95 regressions with the exact config version + rollback timestamp because charts weren’t consistently annotated and most panels lacked config_version breakdown. This is a prerequisite for GA readiness (PM-904) and supports the auditability story for enterprise (SUP-18544 follow-up: 'prove rollback happened at X time and the metrics recovered'). What’s included: - Annotations: Adds an annotation query that pulls Optimization Config events from the control plane event stream (exported into Loki) and renders vertical markers for apply/promote/rollback, scoped by tenant/model/region. Events are labeled with config_name, config_version, actor, and rollout_id. - Panels (new): (a) Error rate (5xx) by config_version, (b) p95/p99 latency by config_version, (c) Throughput + queue depth, (d) GPU utilization + KV cache hit rate, all with templated variables tenant/model/region and a new variable optimization_config_version. - Panels (updated): Existing "Overall health" panels now include legend formatting to surface config_version when present and fall back gracefully for older workers that don’t emit the tag yet. - Templating: Adds variables for rollout_id and config_version with sensible defaults; when unset, panels aggregate across versions as before. - Documentation: Updates dashboard README to document required metric tags (tenant, model, region, optimization_config_version) and the minimum versions of runtime/control-plane exporters required for annotations to populate. Compatibility/rollout notes: - Metrics: Panels use the new tag name "optimization_config_version" (per the metrics spec agreed with platform/runtime). If a cluster is still on older exporters, those series simply won’t appear in the by-version panels; the aggregate panels remain usable. - Logs/annotations: Annotation query relies on control-plane events being shipped to Loki under stream {app="control-plane", component="opt-config"}. Private deployments can wire the same annotation by shipping audit/event logs, but it’s optional; doc notes included. Testing: - Verified dashboards render locally in grafana-dev with the sample Prometheus/Loki datasets. - Ran jsonnet compilation + grafana-lint in CI. - Smoke-checked templating and annotation query performance (kept to 24h default with user-controlled range). Screenshots: Attached in the PR UI: 1) Annotated latency chart showing apply->promote->rollback markers, 2) Error rate panel split by optimization_config_version, 3) Variable dropdowns for config_version/rollout_id. Follow-ups (not in scope): - Add a "gate decision" panel that displays the evaluated gate status over time (pending signal from slo-toolkit integration). - Add a compact "Rollback summary" row for on-call (MTTD/MTTR inputs) once incident-bot emits incident_id tags.
commits: dashboards: add optimization config event annotations (apply/promote/rollback)
dashboards: add latency/error panels split by optimization_config_version
dashboards: add saturation panels (gpu util, queue depth) + kv cache hit rate
dashboards: update templating variables (tenant/model/region/config_version/rollout_id)
docs: update dashboard README with tag requirements and compatibility notes
chore: format + grafana-lint fixes
review_conversation: Sean Gallagher: Annotation query looks good, but can we avoid a hard dependency on rollout_id? During incidents we often only know tenant/model/region. Also, please ensure queries don’t go unbounded by default.

Allison Grant (author): Good call. Rollout ID is optional; scoping is tenant/model/region first, with rollout_id as an additional filter. Default dashboard time range is 24h and annotation query uses $__range with a hard cap in the query. Pushed an update.
Benji Okafor: For event labels, can we include actor + request_id? It helps when we’re matching audit logs during investigations (SUP-18544).

Allison Grant (author): Added actor and request_id to the annotation text. request_id is present for API-triggered operations; console-triggered uses the same request_id from backend. If missing, we display "request_id=unknown".
Aisha Bello: The new by-version panels are great. One concern: legend cardinality could explode if someone rolls versions frequently. Can we cap the series or encourage filtering?
Allison Grant (author): Added a note in the README + set the variable to default to "current" (latest seen in range) rather than "all". Users can still select "all" for debugging.
Felix Schneider: Can we match the naming used in Console ("Optimization Config Version") for the variable label? Also, consider adding a link panel to the config history page.

Allison Grant (author): Renamed variable label to "Optimization Config Version". Added a dashboard link to Console history view using tenant/model/region parameters (behind feature flag; link shows even if the page 404s in older envs).
Sean Gallagher: Approved. This will help a lot for gameday and on-call.
Aisha Bello: Approved.
source.release_notes: Dashboards: Optimization Rollout Health now includes annotations for config apply/promote/rollback events and panels to compare error/latency/saturation by optimization_config_version.

Events are labeled with config_name, config_version, actor, and rollout_id. - Panels (new): (a) Error rate (5xx) by config_version, (b) p95/p99 latency by config_version, (c) Throughput + queue depth, (d) GPU utilization + KV cache hit rate, all with templated variables tenant/model/region and a new variable optimization_config_version. - Panels (updated): Existing "Overall health" panels now include legend formatting to surface config_version when present and fall back gracefully for older workers that don’t emit the tag yet. - Templating: Adds variables for rollout_id and config_version with sensible defaults; when unset, panels aggregate across versions as before. - Documentation: Updates dashboard README to document required metric tags (tenant, model, region, optimization_config_version) and the minimum versions of runtime/control-plane exporters required for annotations to populate. Compatibility/rollout notes: - Metrics: Panels use the new tag name "optimization_config_version" (per the metrics spec agreed with platform/runtime). If a cluster is still on older exporters, those series simply won’t appear in the by-version panels; the aggregate panels remain usable. - Logs/annotations: Annotation query relies on control-plane events being shipped to Loki under stream {app="control-plane", component="opt-config"}. Private deployments can wire the same annotation by shipping audit/event logs, but it’s optional; doc notes included. Testing: - Verified dashboards render locally in grafana-dev with the sample Prometheus/Loki datasets. - Ran jsonnet compilation + grafana-lint in CI. - Smoke-checked templating and annotation query performance (kept to 24h default with user-controlled range). Screenshots: Attached in the PR UI: 1) Annotated latency chart showing apply->promote->rollback markers, 2) Error rate panel split by optimization_config_version, 3) Variable dropdowns for config_version/rollout_id. Follow-ups (not in scope): - Add a "gate decision" panel that displays the evaluated gate status over time (pending signal from slo-toolkit integration). - Add a compact "Rollback summary" row for on-call (MTTD/MTTR inputs) once incident-bot emits incident_id tags.

source.title: perf-canary dashboards: add per-model p95 latency + tokens/sec panels (with breakdowns + runbook links)
source.author: Allison Grant
source.description:
## Summary
Adds the initial Grafana dashboard panels for the production performance canary. The goal is fast triage for per-model regressions (p95 latency and tokens/sec) with enough breakdowns to distinguish kernel/runtime execution regressions from scheduler/queueing and infra issues.

This PR is dashboards-only (no alert rules). Alerts are tracked separately so we can iterate on panel queries/labels before we encode anything into paging logic.

## What’s included
- New dashboard: **Perf Canary / Per-model Performance (Prod)**
  - Topline panels per selected model:
    - End-to-end latency p50/p95/p99 (histogram quantiles)
    - Runtime-only latency p50/p95/p99 (when available)
    - Tokens/sec (overall) + tokens/sec (decode-only) where emitted
  - Breakdown panels:
    - Prefill latency vs decode latency (stacked / side-by-side)
    - Queueing time (scheduler) and queueing fraction of end-to-end
    - Batch size distribution (p50/p95 + histogram heatmap)
    - KV cache hit rate (if the metric is present for the target model/runtime)
  - Correlation / attribution helpers:
    - Runtime build tag and kernel selection tag (as table + annotation)
    - GPU hardware SKU selector and region selector (to isolate fleet changes)
  - Drilldowns:
    - Explore link to raw series (preserves template vars)
    - Trace search link (where trace_id exemplars exist) for recent spikes

## Template variables (kept intentionally small)
- `model` (required)
- `region` (defaults to `us-east`, multi-select enabled)
- `tier` (hosted|dedicated|private, defaults to `hosted`)
- `hardware_sku` (optional; hidden by default to avoid casual cardinality blowups)
- `runtime_build` (optional; used primarily during rollouts)

Note: we do **not** template on `prompt_shape` / `bucket` even though canary emits shape buckets. Those panels are included as a separate collapsed row (opt-in) because we saw the label surface expand quickly during early experiments.

## Metric/query notes
- Uses the canonical perf-canary metric names agreed in ENG-4873:
  - `perf_canary_request_latency_seconds_bucket` (e2e)
  - `perf_canary_runtime_latency_seconds_bucket` (runtime-only)
  - `perf_canary_tokens_per_second` (gauge)
  - `perf_canary_queueing_seconds_bucket` (scheduler)
  - `perf_canary_batch_size` (histogram)
  - `perf_canary_kv_cache_hit_ratio` (gauge)
- Histogram quantiles are computed with `histogram_quantile()` over `rate(<bucket>[5m])`.
- Tokens/sec uses `avg_over_time(...[5m])` with a 1m min step to reduce graph jitter.
- All panels include `model`, `region`, and `tier` in the legend; other dimensions are shown only when explicitly filtered.

## Runbook links
Each top-level row includes a link to the on-call runbook for canary regressions:
- Confluence: `eng-sre/runbooks/runbook-perf-regression-canary-alert`
- The link is parameterized via dashboard variables (model/region/tier) so the runbook opens with context in the URL.

## Screenshots
- Attached in the PR UI (see: “Per-model overview row”, “Breakdown row”, “Annotations + drilldowns”).

## Rollout plan
- Land in observability-pack, then enable in `prod` Grafana folder via the normal dashboard sync job.
- Runtime/SRE will sanity check the panels during Phase 1 rollout (us-east, 2–3 models).

## Testing
- `make dashboards-lint`
- `make dashboards-render` (jsonnet render)
- Verified in staging Grafana against canary metrics (us-east) for: `llama-3.1-70b-instruct` and `qwen2.5-32b-instruct`.

## Follow-ups
- Add annotation feed for control-plane canary model list changes (so changes in coverage are obvious on the graphs).
- Add a small “alert preview” row (not rules) showing computed deviation vs baseline once baseline store is stable.

Refs: ENG-4877, ENG-4873
commits: dashboards: add perf-canary per-model dashboard scaffold
dashboards: add latency quantiles + tokens/sec panels
dashboards: add breakdown row (prefill/decode/queueing) + batch size
dashboards: add KV cache hit rate panel (guarded by metric existence)
dashboards: add runbook + explore drilldown links
dashboards: tidy legends + enforce variable defaults and limits
review_conversation: Logan Wright: Can we ensure we’re not encouraging people to template on high-cardinality labels (prompt_bucket, seed, request_id)? Also please add a note about `hardware_sku` being hidden by default.

Allison Grant (author): Yep. I hid `hardware_sku` behind an “Advanced” toggle and I’m not exposing prompt bucket as a template var; it’s a collapsed row with fixed queries. Added README note on label guidance + why.
Nadia Rahman: The p95 panels look good. One tweak: can we standardize the histogram window to 5m across the board? I see 1m in a couple queries which will be noisy at low QPS.
Allison Grant (author): Good catch. Updated all histogram quantiles to `rate(bucket[5m])`. Left tokens/sec as `avg_over_time([5m])` for smoothing.
Noah Patel: Please add a panel showing runtime_build + kernel_tag as a table so when a regression hits we don’t have to click around. Also, the runbook link should include model + region at minimum.

Allison Grant (author): Added a small table panel in the header row with `runtime_build`, `kernel_tag`, and `model_version` (when present). Updated runbook and Explore links to carry `var-model` + `var-region` + `var-tier`.

Aisha Bello: Can you align the dashboard naming + folder path with the SRE conventions? Also please add a short “how to use this dashboard” blurb at the top so oncall isn’t guessing what panels matter first.
Allison Grant (author): Renamed to `Perf Canary / Per-model Performance (Prod)` and added a dashboard description block (top). Folder path is now `Prod/Perf Canary` per convention.
Caleb Johnson: For the trace link, is this safe if traces don’t have exemplars? I’d rather have it degrade gracefully than show a broken link.

Allison Grant (author): It’s a soft link (panel link) to the trace search page filtered by model/region/time range; no exemplar dependency. I removed the exemplar-specific wording from the dashboard text.
Logan Wright: Looks good. Approving once CI is green.
CI: dashboards-render failed (jsonnet) due to missing import path in `links.libsonnet`.
Allison Grant (author): Fixed import path; reran CI.
CI: pass.
Logan Wright: Approved.
Noah Patel: Approved.
Aisha Bello: Approved.
source.release_notes: Adds initial perf-canary per-model Grafana dashboard panels (p95 latency, tokens/sec, breakdowns) with runbook and drilldown links.

source.title: Reduce cardinality for incident_type metric label (allowlist + sampling + unknown buckets)
source.author: Sergio Costa
source.description: Context: PR #1459 introduced incident taxonomy dashboards and alert rules. During rollout review, Observability flagged that naively labeling high-volume request/edge metrics with incident_type can explode series cardinality (especially when incident-bot classification is missing/late, or when teams attempt to encode subtypes/notes). This follow-up tightens the metric labeling contract and adjusts recording rules to keep the dashboards useful without putting Prometheus/Mimir under stress. What changed: 1) Added an explicit allowlist for incident_type values used in metrics (top-level types only, no freeform). 2) Introduced two stable buckets for missing/invalid classification: incident_type="unknown" (not set) and incident_type="unclassified" (set but not in allowlist / failed validation). 3) Moved incident_type labeling off the highest-cardinality per-route/per-model series and onto lower-cardinality rollups used by the dashboards (recording rules). 4) Added a conservative sampling strategy for the remaining labeled raw series (only for error events and only for a limited set of metrics), with clear comments and defaults. 5) Updated dashboard queries and alert annotations to treat unknown/unclassified explicitly and to keep them visible (we want to drive down unknowns, not hide them). Guidance / policy baked into this repo: - Metrics must only use the top-level incident_type enum (no subtype) and must not include user/customer identifiers. - If classification is missing at emission time, emit incident_type="unknown". If a value is present but not in allowlist, normalize to incident_type="unclassified" and emit a counter for visibility. - Dashboards should break out unknown/unclassified as first-class categories so SRE can track adoption and decision-tree gaps. Rollout notes: This should be safe to deploy independently of incident-bot changes. It assumes the incident_type field exists in log/metric enrichment but tolerates it being absent. After merge, please re-run the cardinality check job in staging and confirm series counts stay within the thresholds in docs/metrics/cardinality.md. Related: ENG-4826 (metric label guardrails), ENG-4828 (dashboard build), and the earlier dashboard PR #1459.
commits: metrics: add incident_type allowlist + normalization (unknown/unclassified)
rules: shift incident taxonomy panels to recording-rule rollups
alerts: annotate unknown/unclassified and adjust grouping keys
docs: document cardinality contract and sampling defaults
ci: add cardinality check budget for incident taxonomy rules
source.illustrative_snippet: normalize_incident_type(value): if value == null || value == "" then "unknown" else if std.member(value, incident_type_allowlist) then value else "unclassified"; // NOTE: do not use subtype or freeform tags in metrics.
review_conversation: Allison Grant (review): We need a hard contract that incident_type is top-level only. Subtype belongs in incident tooling/events, not metrics. Please add doc language and enforce normalization to unknown/unclassified. Also please confirm dashboards still show unknown explicitly so we can track compliance.

Sergio Costa (author): Added docs/metrics/incident-type-labeling.md with the contract + examples; updated normalization to bucket missing as unknown and invalid as unclassified; updated dashboards to render unknown/unclassified as separate series and added an 'Unknown rate' stat panel.
Aisha Bello (review): Recording rules look good, but the alert grouping key still includes route in one place, which defeats the point. Can we group by tier + incident_type + owner only?
Sergio Costa (author): Good catch. Removed route/model from grouping; kept them in annotations via topk query so the page is still actionable without exploding series.
Rafael Mendes (review): Please add a metric/counter for 'normalized_to_unclassified' so we can chase down emitters. Also add a note on how incident-bot should behave if it can’t determine a type.

Sergio Costa (author): Added redwood_observability_incident_type_normalization_total{reason="unclassified"|"unknown"} and a short guidance section: emit unknown when unset; do not invent new values; add to allowlist via PR if needed.
Mei Lin (review): Can we add a simple before/after estimate for series count impact? Helpful for the program update.

Sergio Costa (author): Added numbers from staging replay in docs/metrics/cardinality.md: incident taxonomy dashboards now rely on 18 rollup series per tier vs ~400+ in the raw per-route view. Included the command to reproduce.
source.release_notes: Internal-only: Tightened incident_type metric labeling to prevent cardinality blowups; dashboards/alerts now treat unknown/unclassified explicitly and rely on lower-cardinality rollups.
